fonts.adobeHelp: Enter your Adobe Fonts (Typekit) project ID to discover available fonts. You can find it at fonts.adobe.com/my_fonts > Web Projects.

fonts.emptyDescription: Add custom font families to use them in your themes.
fonts.googleHelp: Add Google Fonts beyond the curated list. Enter the exact font family name from fonts.google.com.
fonts.googleSpecHint: Use format "Family Name:weights", e.g. "Raleway:400,700" or just "Raleway".
fonts.monotypeHelp: Enter your fonts.com (Monotype) project ID and the CSS font-family name. The project ID can be found in your fonts.com Web Fonts project settings.

settings.analytics.description: External analytics complement built-in insights by tracking cross-site visitor journeys. Choose privacy-friendly providers that respect your visitors.
settings.analytics.envVarsNote: Analytics can also be configured via environment variables. Settings configured here will override environment variables.
settings.analytics.privacyFriendlyDesc: These providers are designed with privacy in mind. They typically do not use cookies and are GDPR-compliant by default.
settings.analytics.providers.googleAnaly: Google's analytics platform. Widely used but sets cookies.
Found in GA4 > Admin > Data Streams > your stream
Google Analytics sets cookies and shares data with Google. You should display a cookie consent banner and update your privacy policy when using this provider.
settings.analytics.providers.matomo.desc: Full-featured, self-hostable analytics with cookie-free tracking mode.
settings.analytics.providers.matomo.requ: Wait for explicit user consent before tracking
settings.analytics.providers.matomo.site: Found in Matomo under Settings > Websites > Manage
settings.analytics.providers.plausible.d: Lightweight, cookie-free analytics. EU-hosted cloud option available.
The domain you want to track
settings.analytics.providers.plausible.u: Leave empty for Plausible Cloud, or enter your self-hosted URL
settings.analytics.providers.umami.descr: Cookie-free, GDPR compliant, open-source analytics. Can be self-hosted.
settings.analytics.providers.umami.urlHi: Leave empty for Umami Cloud, or enter your self-hosted URL
settings.analytics.providers.umami.websi: Found in your Umami dashboard under Settings > Websites
settings.analytics.requiresConsentDesc: These providers set cookies and require a consent banner under GDPR/ePrivacy. Consider privacy-friendly alternatives above.
settings.analytics.validation.enabledBut: Provider is enabled but required fields are missing. It won't be active until configured.

settings.mcp.description: Deckyard speaks the Model Context Protocol: point Claude Desktop, Cursor, or a remote agent at this server to create and edit decks in natural language. Remote access authenticates with one of the API keys above.

settings.mcp.desktop.label: Claude Desktop / Cursor (local)
settings.mcp.remote.help: Endpoint /mcp. Create a key above and send it as a Bearer token.
settings.mcp.remote.label: Remote agents (HTTP + API key)
settings.mcp.rest.label: Prefer plain REST?
settings.mcp.title: Connect via API / MCP

settings.privacy.disableTracking: Opt out of all engagement tracking

settings.slideTypes.baseTypeHint: Optional. The core type this custom type extends. Used for fallback rendering.
settings.slideTypes.cssHint: Scoped CSS injected into the slide rendering context.
settings.slideTypes.curationDescription: Control which core slide types are available for new slides. Disabling a type hides it from the picker but existing slides remain unaffected.
settings.slideTypes.defaultsHint: Default field values for new slides of this type.
settings.slideTypes.deleteConfirm: Delete custom type "{label}"? This cannot be undone.
settings.slideTypes.description: Create custom slide types and control which types are available in the slide picker.
settings.slideTypes.errorFieldsRequired: At least one field is required.
settings.slideTypes.errorInvalidSlug: Invalid slug. Use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
settings.slideTypes.fields.empty: No fields defined. Add fields to define the slide content structure.
settings.slideTypes.fields.label: Label
settings.slideTypes.help.bgClass: Turns a background choice into the matching slide background class.
settings.slideTypes.help.each: Repeats the block for every entry of an items field.
settings.slideTypes.help.esc: The value of a field, HTML-escaped.
settings.slideTypes.help.if: Only renders the first part when the field has a value. Empty text, zero, false and empty lists all count as no value.
settings.slideTypes.help.index: Inside {{#each}}: the position of the current entry, starting at 0.
settings.slideTypes.help.markdown: The field rendered as markdown (bold, italics, links, lists).
settings.slideTypes.help.noFields: This type has no fields yet — add some above and their names appear here.
settings.slideTypes.help.raw: The field inserted as-is, without escaping. Only for values you control.
settings.slideTypes.help.this: Inside {{#each}}: the current entry, or one of its sub-fields.
settings.slideTypes.help.title: Template syntax
settings.slideTypes.help.var: The value of a field, HTML-escaped. Same as {{esc field}}.
settings.slideTypes.importInvalid: That file is not a valid slide type export.
settings.slideTypes.importSuccess: Slide type imported as a draft.
settings.slideTypes.loadError: Failed to load slide type settings.
settings.slideTypes.name: Name
settings.slideTypes.noCustomTypesHint: Create a custom slide type to define your own layout with fields, templates, and CSS.
settings.slideTypes.preview.title: Slide type preview
settings.slideTypes.publishHint: Draft types are only visible in settings. Published types appear in the slide picker for all users.
settings.slideTypes.slugHint: Auto-generated from name. Used as the internal type key.
settings.slideTypes.slugReadonly: Slug cannot be changed after creation.
settings.slideTypes.title: Slide Types
settings.themes.colorsHint: Define your brand colors. Accent colors are derived from the primary color.
settings.themes.config.backgroundsFull: That is as many background images as a theme can hold.
settings.themes.config.backgroundsHint: Your own imagery for title slides. New title slides pick one of these, and the per-slide picker offers them first. Without any, title slides stay flat rather than borrowing imagery that is not yours.
settings.themes.config.backgroundsSomeSk: Only {count} more images fit; the rest were skipped.
settings.themes.config.builtinsHint: Every theme has two built-in backgrounds. They are storage slots, not colours — name them for what yours actually are, or they show as "Color 1" and "Color 2".
settings.themes.config.locksHint: A locked property is not editable per slide, and an override an existing slide already carries is ignored when it renders. Unlocking gives every slide its own value back.
settings.themes.config.surfacesHint: How rounded and how raised the shapes on a slide feel. Both scale the design system rather than replacing it.
settings.themes.config.typographyHint: Applies to headings across every slide type. The fonts themselves are set above.
settings.themes.config.variantBadName: A name needs at least one letter or digit.
settings.themes.config.variantDuplicate: You already have a variant with that name.
settings.themes.config.variantReserved: That name is taken by a built-in background.
settings.themes.config.variantsFull: That is as many background options as a theme can hold.
settings.themes.config.variantsHint: Extra background choices on top of the built-in ones. Each appears in the Background picker on every slide, and its text colour follows automatically so a dark option stays readable.
settings.themes.deleteConfirm: Delete theme "{label}"? This cannot be undone.
settings.themes.description: Create and manage custom themes for your organization. Custom themes define colors, fonts, and logos for presentations.
settings.themes.dropLogoHere: Drop logo here or click to upload
settings.themes.fontsHint: Choose fonts for headings and body text.
settings.themes.logoHint: Upload your logo image. SVG format recommended for best quality.
settings.themes.logoSmallHint: Smaller logo for title slides. Uses main logo if not set.
settings.themes.noThemesHint: Create a custom theme to define your organization's brand colors and fonts.
settings.themes.preview.body:
- Body text in the body font
- A second point
- A third point
settings.themes.preview.quote: A quote, on the theme’s dark surface.
settings.themes.preview.subtitle: A subtitle, in the body font
settings.themes.preview.title: Your presentation title
settings.themes.themeNameHint: Give your theme a descriptive name.
settings.themes.workspace.hint: Set the theme new presentations start with, and which themes appear in the picker up front. Hidden themes stay reachable behind "Show all themes".
settings.themes.workspace.title: Theme picker
settings.themes.workspace.visibleHint: Check the themes shown up front. The default theme is always visible. With everything checked, new themes are visible automatically.
settings.title: Settings
settings.uiLocale.hint: The language of this application's interface, unrelated to the language used in slides.
settings.uiLocale.title: Application language
